openmeter/notification/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL persistence layer for the notification domain, implementing notification.Repository against four Ent entities (NotificationChannel, NotificationRule, NotificationEvent, NotificationEventDeliveryStatus). Every method body is wrapped in entutils.TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue so the ctx-carried Ent transaction is honored.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapper on every method
patterns.description: All adapter methods define an inner fn closure and pass it to entutils.TransactingRepo (value returns) or entutils.TransactingRepoWithNoValue (error-only). Never call a.db directly outside a fn wrapper.
patterns.example: fn := func(ctx context.Context, a *adapter) (*notification.Channel, error) { ... }; return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, fn)

patterns.name: Soft-delete via SetDisabled + SetDeletedAt
patterns.description: Deletion of channels and rules is a soft-delete: SetDisabled(true) + SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()). All list queries filter with channeldb.Or(channeldb.DeletedAtIsNil(), channeldb.DeletedAtGT(clock.Now())) — never hard-delete.
patterns.example: a.db.NotificationChannel.UpdateOneID(params.ID).SetDisabled(true).SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()).Save(ctx)

patterns.name: EagerLoadActiveChannels / EagerLoadRulesWithActiveChannels
patterns.description: Shared query-modifier functions (in rule.go and event.go) filter channels to non-disabled and non-deleted at a given time. Always use these when loading rule.Edges.Channels or event.Edges.Rules.
patterns.example: query.WithChannels(EagerLoadActiveChannels(clock.Now()))

patterns.name: EntityMapping functions in entitymapping.go
patterns.description: All DB-to-domain conversions go through pure functions: ChannelFromDBEntity, RuleFromDBEntity, EventFromDBEntity, EventDeliveryStatusFromDBEntity. Never map inline in CRUD methods.
patterns.example: return ChannelFromDBEntity(*channel), nil

patterns.name: entdb.IsNotFound -> notification.NotFoundError
patterns.description: After any query, check entdb.IsNotFound(err) and return notification.NotFoundError{NamespacedID: models.NamespacedID{Namespace:..., ID:...}} — never return raw Ent errors.
patterns.example: if entdb.IsNotFound(err) { return nil, notification.NotFoundError{NamespacedID: models.NamespacedID{Namespace: params.Namespace, ID: params.ID}} }

patterns.name: Bulk delivery-status creation inside CreateEvent transaction
patterns.description: CreateEvent saves the event, re-queries the rule with EagerLoadActiveChannels, then calls CreateBulk to insert one NotificationEventDeliveryStatus per active channel — all inside the same TransactingRepo fn closure.
patterns.example: statusBulkQuery := make([]*entdb.NotificationEventDeliveryStatusCreate, 0, len(ruleRow.Edges.Channels)); ... a.db.NotificationEventDeliveryStatus.CreateBulk(statusBulkQuery...).Save(ctx)

patterns.name: Payload serialized as JSON string column
patterns.description: NotificationEvent.Payload is stored as a string. CreateEvent calls json.Marshal(params.Payload) before SetPayload; EventFromDBEntity calls eventPayloadFromJSON which validates EventPayloadMeta.Version for invoice-type events.
patterns.example: payloadJSON, err := json.Marshal(params.Payload); query.SetPayload(string(payloadJSON))

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config (Client, Logger), New() constructor, adapter struct with Tx/WithTx/Self for entutils transaction plumbing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithTx creates a new txClient from raw config via entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig — must stay in sync with entutils.TxDriver contract. Self() is required by the TransactingRepo generic constraint.

key_file_guides.role: Pure DB-to-domain mapping functions. eventPayloadFromJSON performs version check for invoice events and rejects unknown versions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new EventType must be handled in the eventPayloadFromJSON switch or it silently deserializes without version check. JSON shape tests in entitymapping_test.go guard against embedding regressions.

key_file_guides.role: ListEvents and CreateEvent — most complex file. CreateEvent is a multi-step operation: save event, fetch rule+channels, bulk-create delivery statuses — all in one TransactingRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If a new event type is added, delivery status creation logic in CreateEvent must handle nil channel edges. EagerLoadRulesWithActiveChannels is exported for reuse in list queries.

key_file_guides.role: CRUD for NotificationRule. UpdateRule calls ClearChannels().AddChannelIDs() to replace the channel set atomically. EagerLoadActiveChannels defined here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateRule and UpdateRule both re-query channels after Save to populate Edges.Channels — required because Ent Save does not return edge data.

key_file_guides.role: CRUD for NotificationChannel with orderBy switch and pagination.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IncludeDisabled=false adds channeldb.Disabled(false) filter; missing this would leak disabled channels.

key_file_guides.role: ListEventsDeliveryStatus, GetEventDeliveryStatus, UpdateEventDeliveryStatus — all wrapped in TransactingRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpdateEventDeliveryStatus uses SetOrClearNextAttemptAt for nullable NextAttemptAt — do not use SetNextAttemptAt which would panic on nil.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db directly outside a TransactingRepo/TransactingRepoWithNoValue fn closure — bypasses ctx transaction
Returning raw entdb errors instead of notification.NotFoundError for not-found cases
Mapping DB rows inline in CRUD methods instead of using entitymapping.go helper functions
Loading rule channels without EagerLoadActiveChannels — would include disabled or deleted channels
Manually editing eventPayloadFromJSON to skip version validation for new invoice event types

decisions.decision: Soft-delete instead of hard-delete for channels and rules
decisions.rationale: In-flight events reference rule and channel IDs; hard deletion would break foreign key integrity and orphan delivery status records.

decisions.decision: Bulk delivery-status insert inside CreateEvent transaction
decisions.rationale: Atomicity: an event with no delivery statuses would never be dispatched; creating them in the same TX prevents a window where the event exists but has no pending deliveries.

decisions.decision: eventPayloadFromJSON version check only for invoice event types
decisions.rationale: Invoice payload schema changed; version 0 (missing version field) must be rejected to prevent deserializing stale JSONB into the new InvoicePayload shape.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new adapter method returning a domain value inside a transaction

openmeter/notification/consumer.purpose: Watermill-based Kafka consumer for the notification service, routing system-topic events (entitlement snapshots, billing invoice created/updated) to typed handlers that call notification.Service.CreateEvent. Primary constraint: each new event type requires a new grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler registration in consumer.go.

patterns.name: grouphandler.NewNoPublishingHandler with typed GroupEventHandlers
patterns.description: consumer.go wires all event types in a single NewNoPublishingHandler call, each wrapped in grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler[T]. Unknown event types are silently dropped.
patterns.example: grouphandler.NewNoPublishingHandler(opts.Marshaler, opts.Router.MetricMeter, grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler(func(ctx context.Context, event *snapshot.SnapshotEvent) error { return consumer.entitlementSnapshotHandler.Handle(ctx, *event) }), ...)

patterns.name: EntitlementSnapshotHandler dispatches both threshold and reset
patterns.description: entitlementsnapshot.go routes to handleAsSnapshotEvent (balance threshold) and handleAsEntitlementResetEvent (reset) based on isBalanceThresholdEvent / isEntitlementResetEvent predicates. Both paths call notification.Service.CreateEvent.
patterns.example: func (b *EntitlementSnapshotHandler) Handle(ctx context.Context, event snapshot.SnapshotEvent) error { if b.isBalanceThresholdEvent(event) { ... }; if b.isEntitlementResetEvent(event) { ... } }

patterns.name: Dual V1+V2 dedup hash query for balance threshold events
patterns.description: Balance threshold events are deduplicated by querying ListEvents with DeduplicationHashes containing both V1 (sha256) and V2 (xxh3 with ThresholdKind prefix). New events are only created when no matching hash exists in the current usage period.
patterns.example: dedupHash, err := NewBalanceEventDedupHash(balSnapshot, rule.ID, *threshold); lastEvents, _ := b.Notification.ListEvents(ctx, notification.ListEventsInput{DeduplicationHashes: []string{dedupHash.V1(), dedupHash.V2()}})

patterns.name: EventPayloadVersionCurrent required for invoice payloads
patterns.description: Invoice notification.EventPayload must always include EventPayloadMeta{Type: eventType, Version: notification.EventPayloadVersionCurrent}. Missing Version causes adapter deserialization to reject the payload.
patterns.example: payload := notification.EventPayload{EventPayloadMeta: notification.EventPayloadMeta{Type: eventType, Version: notification.EventPayloadVersionCurrent}, Invoice: &notification.InvoicePayload{Invoice: apiInvoice}}

patterns.name: Annotation-based event metadata
patterns.description: All created events carry annotations (AnnotationEventSubjectKey, AnnotationEventFeatureKey, AnnotationEventCustomerID, AnnotationBalanceEventDedupeHash) set from the snapshot/invoice payload before calling CreateEvent.
patterns.example: annotations := models.Annotations{notification.AnnotationEventSubjectKey: in.Snapshot.Subject.Key, notification.AnnotationBalanceEventDedupeHash: in.DedupeHash}

patterns.name: Invoice handler skips gathering invoices
patterns.description: InvoiceEventHandler.Handle checks event.Invoice.Status == billing.StandardInvoiceStatusGathering and returns nil early — gathering invoices do not produce notification events.
patterns.example: if event.Invoice.Status == billing.StandardInvoiceStatusGathering { return nil }

key_file_guides.role: Wires the Watermill router, instantiates handler structs, registers all event type handlers. Run(ctx) starts the router; Close() stops it.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new event type requires adding a new grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler here; forgetting it means the event is silently ignored.

key_file_guides.role: Dispatcher: calls handleAsSnapshotEvent (balance threshold) and handleAsEntitlementResetEvent (reset). Both can trigger on the same SnapshotEvent.
key_file_guides.watch_for: isBalanceThresholdEvent gates processing — non-metered entitlements and delete operations are skipped; event.Value must not be nil.

key_file_guides.role: Core balance threshold logic: rule filtering, dedup hash computation (V1/V2), last-event comparison, event creation. Contains BalanceEventDedupHash, getNumericThreshold, getActiveThresholdsWithHighestPriority.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Percentage thresholds require TotalAvailableGrantAmount > 0; if grants are zero, ErrNoBalanceAvailable is returned and event creation is skipped. ThresholdKind is part of the V2 hash — changing the kind mapping is a breaking dedup change.

key_file_guides.role: Handles entitlement reset events: lists rules of type EventTypeEntitlementReset, deduplicates by checking for existing events in the current period, creates EntitlementReset payloads.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Dedup here checks for any event in the current usage period — simpler than balance threshold dedup, but still scoped to the period.

key_file_guides.role: Handles billing.StandardInvoiceCreatedEvent and StandardInvoiceUpdatedEvent. Calls billinghttp.MapEventInvoiceToAPI to get the API invoice, then creates one notification event per active rule.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Gathering invoices are skipped explicitly. Rules with Disabled=true are also skipped with a warning log.

anti_patterns: Adding a new event type handler without registering it in consumer.go's NewNoPublishingHandler call — event is silently dropped
Setting Version field to anything other than notification.EventPayloadVersionCurrent in invoice EventPayloadMeta
Changing BalanceEventDedupHash field composition without migrating V1/V2 hash constants — breaks dedup for existing events
Calling notification.Service methods without propagating the Watermill message context (msg.Context())
Triggering notification events for gathering invoices (Status == StandardInvoiceStatusGathering)

decisions.decision: Dual V1+V2 dedup hash query for balance threshold events
decisions.rationale: V2 adds ThresholdKind to the hash to prevent usage and balance threshold events from aliasing each other; V1 is queried alongside for backward compatibility with events created before V2 existed.

decisions.decision: activeThresholds struct separates Usage and Balance thresholds
decisions.rationale: Usage (value/percent) and balance (remaining balance) thresholds are semantically independent; the highest active threshold from each kind is selected separately so both types can fire concurrently.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new system-event handler to the consumer

openmeter/notification/httpdriver.purpose: HTTP handler layer for the notification v1 API (channels, rules, events, delivery status), implementing httptransport.Handler[Req,Resp] and HandlerWithArgs patterns. Delegates all business logic to notification.Service; only responsible for request decoding, response encoding, and error mapping.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs for every endpoint
patterns.description: Each CRUD operation returns a typed Handler or HandlerWithArgs. The decoder extracts namespace via h.resolveNamespace(ctx) and maps API params to service Input types. The operation calls h.service.<Method>. The encoder uses commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus.
patterns.example: return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoderFn, operationFn, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[ListChannelsResponse](http.StatusOK), httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("listNotificationChannels"), httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()))...)

patterns.name: Type alias pattern for Request/Response/Params per endpoint
patterns.description: Each handler file declares type aliases: <Verb><Noun>Request = notification.<Verb><Noun>Input, <Verb><Noun>Response = api.<ResponseType>, <Verb><Noun>Handler = httptransport.Handler[...]. Keeps signatures self-documenting.
patterns.example: type (ListChannelsRequest = notification.ListChannelsInput; ListChannelsResponse = api.NotificationChannelPaginatedResponse; ListChannelsHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...])

patterns.name: Custom errorEncoder() in errors.go
patterns.description: All handlers pass httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()) in their options. The error encoder in errors.go maps notification domain errors (NotFoundError) to correct HTTP status codes.
patterns.example: httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()))

patterns.name: FromChannel / FromRule / FromEvent mapping functions in mapping.go
patterns.description: All domain-to-API conversions live in mapping.go. Never inline domain-to-API conversion in handler files.
patterns.example: item, err = FromChannel(channel); item, err = FromRule(rule)

patterns.name: namespace resolution via h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
patterns.description: Every decoder calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) to get the tenant namespace — never read namespace from request body or path parameters directly.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ListChannelsRequest{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve namespace: %w", err) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface (all endpoint methods), handler struct (service, options, resolveNamespace), and New() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New endpoints must be added to the Handler interface in handler.go before implementing the method.

key_file_guides.role: errorEncoder function mapping notification domain errors to HTTP status codes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: notification.NotFoundError must map to 404; missing an error type causes it to fall through to 500.

key_file_guides.role: Domain-to-API conversion functions (FromChannel, FromRule, FromEvent, etc.) and API-to-domain conversion helpers (AsChannelWebhookCreateRequest, AsRuleBalanceThresholdCreateRequest, etc.).
key_file_guides.watch_for: mapping_test.go has coverage for payload shape; new payload types need test cases. FromRule dispatches on rule.Type — add a new case for every new EventType.

key_file_guides.role: Rule CRUD handlers plus TestRule handler (generates a synthetic event via internal.TestEventGenerator and calls CreateEvent).
key_file_guides.watch_for: TestRule accesses h.testEventGenerator — ensure it is injected in New() when wiring.

anti_patterns: Putting business logic in decoder/encoder functions instead of delegating to notification.Service
Inlining domain-to-API mapping in handler files instead of using mapping.go functions
Reading namespace from request body instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Omitting httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()) from handler options
Returning non-API types from handler operations — encoder expects api.* response types

decisions.decision: Type aliases for Request/Response/Handler types per endpoint
decisions.rationale: Makes handler signatures self-documenting and enables the router to reference concrete handler types without importing service input types directly.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new list endpoint following the established handler pattern

openmeter/notification/internal.purpose: Internal test-helper package that generates synthetic notification event payloads (balance threshold, entitlement reset, invoice) for use in notification rule tests and test event dispatch — never imported from production code paths.

patterns.name: TestEventGenerator struct with injected billing.Service
patterns.description: The sole exported type wraps billing.Service to generate realistic invoice payloads via billing.SimulateInvoice. Entitlement payloads are built from hardcoded fixture values.
patterns.example: func NewTestEventGenerator(billingService billing.Service) *TestEventGenerator { return &TestEventGenerator{billingService: billingService} }

patterns.name: EventGeneratorInput.Validate() before switching on EventType
patterns.description: Generate() validates the input struct before branching, surfacing empty namespace or event type errors early.
patterns.example: func (t *TestEventGenerator) Generate(ctx context.Context, in EventGeneratorInput) (notification.EventPayload, error) { if err := in.Validate(); err != nil { return notification.EventPayload{}, err }; switch in.EventType { ... } }

patterns.name: Invoice payloads go through billing.SimulateInvoice + billinghttp.MapEventInvoiceToAPI
patterns.description: Rather than constructing invoice API types by hand, newTestInvoicePayload calls the real billing simulation path and maps through the HTTP driver converter — keeping test fixtures aligned with production serialization.
patterns.example: invoice, _ := t.billingService.SimulateInvoice(ctx, billing.SimulateInvoiceInput{...}); eventInvoice, _ := billing.NewEventStandardInvoice(invoice); apiInvoice, _ := billinghttp.MapEventInvoiceToAPI(eventInvoice)

key_file_guides.role: Entire package. Defines TestEventGenerator, EventGeneratorInput, and three private payload builders for the three supported event types (balance threshold, entitlement reset, invoice).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Entitlement/balance payloads use hardcoded ULID strings and timestamps — if notification payload schemas change, update the fixture literals here or test assertions will silently diverge. New event types added to Generate() must also set EventPayloadMeta.Type correctly or callers will misroute.

anti_patterns: Importing this package from production code — it imports billing HTTP driver types that have no place in the live dispatch path
Extending Generate() for a new event type without updating EventPayloadMeta.Type inside the returned payload
Replacing billing.SimulateInvoice with hand-crafted invoice structs — the simulation path exercises real serialization and keeps the test payload structurally valid

decisions.decision: Use billing.SimulateInvoice for invoice test payloads instead of static fixtures
decisions.rationale: Invoice API shape is complex and changes frequently; going through the real simulation + HTTP mapping ensures the test payload is always structurally valid and aligned with production serialization.

openmeter/notification/service.purpose: Concrete implementation of notification.Service, orchestrating channel and rule lifecycle against both the notification.Repository (Ent/PostgreSQL) and the webhook.Handler (Svix). All multi-step mutations use transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue; Svix webhook operations are performed inside the same transaction scope as adapter writes.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps all multi-step mutations
patterns.description: CreateChannel, DeleteChannel, UpdateChannel, CreateRule, DeleteRule, and UpdateRule all wrap their bodies in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, fn) or transaction.RunWithNoValue so adapter calls and webhook calls share a transaction context.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (*notification.Channel, error) { channel, _ := s.adapter.CreateChannel(ctx, params); _, _ = s.webhook.CreateWebhook(ctx, ...); return channel, nil })

patterns.name: params.Validate() called before every mutation
patterns.description: Every Service method calls params.Validate() (or params.ValidateWith(...)) before any adapter or webhook call. Returns fmt.Errorf wrapping the validation error without touching the DB.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid params: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Webhook adapter kept in sync with channel/rule lifecycle
patterns.description: CreateChannel calls webhook.CreateWebhook then adapter.UpdateChannel (to store SigningSecret). DeleteChannel calls webhook.DeleteWebhook before adapter.DeleteChannel. CreateRule calls webhook.UpdateWebhookChannels(AddChannels) per channel; DeleteRule calls UpdateWebhookChannels(RemoveChannels).
patterns.example: wb, err = s.webhook.CreateWebhook(ctx, webhook.CreateWebhookInput{Namespace: params.Namespace, ID: &channel.ID, ...}); updateIn.Config.WebHook.SigningSecret = wb.Secret; channel, err = s.adapter.UpdateChannel(ctx, updateIn)

patterns.name: ValidateRuleChannels generic constraint
patterns.description: ValidateRuleChannels[T] is a generic validator used in CreateRule and UpdateRule that calls s.adapter.ListChannels to verify referenced channels exist in the namespace before saving.
patterns.example: err = params.ValidateWith(ValidateRuleChannels[notification.CreateRuleInput](ctx, s))

patterns.name: ChannelIDsDifference for minimal webhook updates on UpdateRule
patterns.description: UpdateRule computes channelIDsDiff := notification.NewChannelIDsDifference(params.Channels, oldChannelIDs) and only calls webhook.UpdateWebhookChannels for channels in Additions or Removals — skips unchanged channels.
patterns.example: channelIDsDiff := notification.NewChannelIDsDifference(params.Channels, oldChannelIDs); if !channelIDsDiff.HasChanged() { return s.adapter.UpdateRule(ctx, params) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Service struct (adapter, webhook, feature, logger), Config, New() constructor, and ListFeature delegating to feature.FeatureConnector.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New dependencies must be added to Config and validated in New(). Service satisfies notification.Service via var _ notification.Service = (*Service)(nil) compile-time assertion.

key_file_guides.role: Implements ChannelService methods. CreateChannel stores the channel first, then creates the Svix webhook and updates SigningSecret back into the DB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DeleteChannel blocks deletion if the channel is assigned to any rule — returns models.NewGenericValidationError with rule IDs. UpdateChannel validates type change is not attempted.

key_file_guides.role: Implements RuleService methods. UpdateRule computes channel diffs and calls webhook.UpdateWebhookChannels only for changed channel assignments.
key_file_guides.watch_for: webhook.IsMaxChannelsPerWebhookExceededError must be checked after UpdateWebhookChannels; if hit, return models.NewGenericValidationError (not a 500).

anti_patterns: Calling adapter methods for multi-step mutations outside transaction.Run — partial writes if webhook call fails mid-transaction
Skipping params.Validate() before adapter calls — invalid inputs reach the DB
Calling webhook.DeleteWebhook after adapter.DeleteChannel — leaves a dangling Svix webhook if DB delete succeeds but webhook delete fails
Deleting a channel without checking for assigned rules first — leaves rules with references to a deleted channel
Calling webhook.UpdateWebhookChannels for all channels on UpdateRule instead of only the diff — causes spurious Svix API calls

decisions.decision: Webhook operations performed inside the same transaction.Run as adapter writes
decisions.rationale: Channel and rule lifecycle must stay consistent with Svix state; wrapping both in the same transaction scope means a webhook API failure rolls back the DB change rather than leaving them out of sync.

decisions.decision: ChannelIDsDifference for minimal Svix calls on UpdateRule
decisions.rationale: Svix enforces a MaxChannelsPerWebhook limit; calling UpdateWebhookChannels for every channel on every rule update would hit the limit unnecessarily and cause spurious validation errors.

code_examples.scenario: Create a new rule with webhook channel sync inside a transaction
